args.query: SELECT id, createddate, closeddate, accountid FROM "Case";

exec_args.sql: SELECT id, createddate, closeddate, accountid FROM "Case";

result.preview: "Execution failed with exit code 1\npandas._libs.tslibs.parsing.DateParseError: Unknown datetime string format, unable to parse: None, at position 0"

args.code:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

cases_data = pd.read_json(locals()['var_function-call-952280197559556309'])
accounts_data = locals()['var_function-call-11821341817521045911']

cases_df = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
accounts_df = pd.DataFrame(accounts_data)

# Clean IDs by removing '#' and stripping whitespace
cases_df['accountid'] = cases_df['accountid'].str.replace('#', '', regex=False).str.strip()
accounts_df['Id'] = accounts_df['Id'].str.replace('#', '', regex=False).str.strip()

# Convert date columns to datetime objects
cases_df['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases_df['createddate'])
cases_df['closeddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases_df['closeddate'])

# Filter out cases with no closeddate or invalid dates
cases_df = cases_df.dropna(subset=['closeddate'])
cases_df = cases_df[cases_df['closeddate'] > cases_df['createddate']]

# Define the start date for the past 6 quarters
today = datetime(2022, 10, 26)
six_quarters_ago = today - pd.DateOffset(months=18)

# Filter cases within the last 6 quarters
cases_filtered_df = cases_df[(cases_df['createddate'] >= six_quarters_ago) & (cases_df['closeddate'] <= today)]

# Calculate closure time in days
cases_filtered_df['closure_time_days'] = (cases_filtered_df['closeddate'] - cases_filtered_df['createddate']).dt.total_seconds() / (60 * 60 * 24)

# Merge with accounts data to get ShippingState
merged_df = pd.merge(cases_filtered_df, accounts_df, left_on='accountid', right_on='Id', how='inner')

# Calculate average closure time per state
avg_closure_time_per_state = merged_df.groupby('ShippingState')['closure_time_days'].mean().reset_index()

# Find the state with the quickest average closure time
quickest_state = avg_closure_time_per_state.loc[avg_closure_time_per_state['closure_time_days'].idxmin()]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(quickest_state['ShippingState'])

args.code:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

cases_data = pd.read_json(locals()['var_function-call-952280197559556309'])
accounts_data = locals()['var_function-call-11821341817521045911']

cases_df = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
accounts_df = pd.DataFrame(accounts_data)

# Clean IDs by removing '#' and stripping whitespace
cases_df['accountid'] = cases_df['accountid'].str.replace('#', '', regex=False).str.strip()
accounts_df['Id'] = accounts_df['Id'].str.replace('#', '', regex=False).str.strip()

# Convert date columns to datetime objects, coercing errors to NaT
cases_df['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases_df['createddate'], errors='coerce')
cases_df['closeddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases_df['closeddate'], errors='coerce')

# Filter out cases with NaT in createddate or closeddate after coercion
cases_df = cases_df.dropna(subset=['createddate', 'closeddate'])

# Filter out cases where closeddate is before createddate
cases_df = cases_df[cases_df['closeddate'] > cases_df['createddate']]

# Define the start date for the past 6 quarters (18 months) from today's date
today = datetime(2022, 10, 26)
six_quarters_ago = today - pd.DateOffset(months=18)

# Filter cases within the last 6 quarters
cases_filtered_df = cases_df[(cases_df['createddate'] >= six_quarters_ago) & (cases_df['closeddate'] <= today)]

# Calculate closure time in days
cases_filtered_df['closure_time_days'] = (cases_filtered_df['closeddate'] - cases_filtered_df['createddate']).dt.total_seconds() / (60 * 60 * 24)

# Merge with accounts data to get ShippingState
merged_df = pd.merge(cases_filtered_df, accounts_df, left_on='accountid', right_on='Id', how='inner')

# Calculate average closure time per state
avg_closure_time_per_state = merged_df.groupby('ShippingState')['closure_time_days'].mean().reset_index()

# Find the state with the quickest average closure time
quickest_state = avg_closure_time_per_state.loc[avg_closure_time_per_state['closure_time_days'].idxmin()]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(quickest_state['ShippingState']))
